edges.description: MDM2 overexpression degrades p53 and CDK4 overexpression drives RB phosphorylation, together releasing cell-cycle control.
Dysregulated cell-cycle and progenitor signaling sustains an undifferentiated tumor-cell state.
ASC-derived tumor cells fail to complete adipogenic differentiation.
edges.source: Adipocyte Stem Cell Origin and Clonal Evolution

edges.target: FUS::DDIT3 Fusion Oncoprotein in Myxoid Liposarcoma

nodes.description: Well-differentiated and dedifferentiated liposarcomas exhibit the characteristic amplification of chromosome region 12q13-15, which contains the oncogenes MDM2 and CDK4 (and HMGA2). This copy-number amplification is the defining molecular event of the WDLPS/DDLPS lineage and is detected diagnostically by FISH/IHC to distinguish these tumors from benign lipoma.

cell_types: mesenchymal stem cell of adipose tissue

evidence.snippet:
WDLPS and DDLPS both exhibit the characteristic 
amplification of chromosome region 12q13-15, which contains the genes CDK4 and 
MDM2.

nodes.description: Single-cell profiling of paired well-differentiated and dedifferentiated DDLPS components identifies a population of tumor adipocyte stem cells (ASC) resembling adipocyte stromal progenitors of white adipose tissue. These ASC harbor the ancestral genomic alterations shared by both components, indicating that the WD and DD components derive from a common progenitor through clonal evolution.
nodes.id: Adipocyte Stem Cell Origin and Clonal Evolution

evidence.snippet:
We 
identify a population of tumor adipocyte stem cells (ASC) showing striking 
similarities with adipocyte stromal progenitors found in white adipose tissue.

tumor ASC harbor the ancestral genomic alterations of WD and DD 
components, suggesting that both derive from these progenitors following clonal 
evolution.

nodes.description: A hallmark of liposarcoma is the failure of tumor cells to complete normal adipogenic differentiation. In DDLPS, the adipogenic properties of ASC-like tumor cells are actively inhibited by a TGF-beta-high immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment. In myxoid liposarcoma, the FUS::DDIT3 chimeric transcription factor blocks adipocyte differentiation. Restoring differentiation (e.g., with trabectedin in MLS) is therapeutically exploitable.

biological_processes: negative regulation of fat cell differentiation
transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.snippet:
their adipogenic properties 
are inhibited by a TGF-β-high immunosuppressive tumor micro-environment.

nodes.description: CDK4/6 inhibitors (e.g., palbociclib) target the amplified CDK4 driver in WDLPS/DDLPS, blocking RB phosphorylation and G1/S cell-cycle progression. Palbociclib has been evaluated in advanced/metastatic liposarcoma.

evidence.snippet:
The most promising results were shown for CDK4/6 and 
MDM2 inhibitors

nodes.description: Myxoid liposarcoma is driven by the FUS::DDIT3 fusion protein, formed by the t(12;16)(q13;p11) translocation and present in over 90% of cases (a minority instead carry EWSR1::DDIT3). FUS::DDIT3 acts as an aberrant transcription factor that inhibits adipocyte differentiation and promotes tumor growth and invasive migration. Trabectedin detaches FUS-DDIT3 from its DNA binding sites, reactivating adipogenesis - providing the mechanistic basis for its unusual efficacy in this subtype.
nodes.id: FUS::DDIT3 Fusion Oncoprotein in Myxoid Liposarcoma

biological_processes: negative regulation of fat cell differentiation

evidence.snippet:
The FUS::DDIT3 fusion protein, formed by the chromosomal translocation t (12;16) 
(q13;p11), is found in over 90% of myxoid liposarcoma (MLS) cases and is a 
crucial protein in its development.

FUS::DDIT3 inhibits adipocyte 
differentiation and promotes MLS growth and invasive migration by functioning as 
an aberrant transcription factor

Trabectedin elicits a significant 
transcriptional response in MLPS resulting in cellular depletion and 
reactivation of adipogenesis.

nodes.description: MDM2 inhibitors (e.g., brigimadlin/BI 907828, milademetan) target the amplified MDM2 driver in WDLPS/DDLPS, reactivating p53. Multiple phase 2/3 programs (Brightline-1, Brightline-4, milademetan vs trabectedin) have operationalized MDM2 as a therapeutic target in DDLPS.

evidence.snippet:
The most promising results were shown for CDK4/6 and 
MDM2 inhibitors

nodes.description: Trabectedin has unusual effectiveness in myxoid liposarcoma. Mechanistically it detaches the FUS-DDIT3 chimera from its DNA binding sites, restoring adipocyte differentiation.

evidence.snippet:
The marine drug trabectedin has shown unusual effectiveness in the 
treatment of myxoid liposarcoma (MLPS), a liposarcoma characterized by the 
expression of the FUS-DDIT3 chimera.

nodes.description: Amplified MDM2 is an E3 ubiquitin ligase that targets the p53 tumor suppressor for degradation, abrogating p53-mediated apoptosis and cell-cycle arrest; amplified CDK4 phosphorylates RB to drive G1/S cell-cycle progression. The combined loss of these checkpoints permits uncontrolled proliferation and underpins the rationale for MDM2 inhibitors and CDK4/6 inhibitors in WDLPS/DDLPS.

evidence.snippet:
The most promising results were shown for CDK4/6 and 
MDM2 inhibitors
